revision.text:
{{Infobox noble family | name = Ōhesoki no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōhesoki, Ōsoki no Mikoto, Taesoki no Mikoto, Ōhesosoki no Mikoto, Ōhesozoki no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_titles = Ōne (大禰), [[Minister (ancient Japan)|Ōomi]] | lord = [[Kōgen Emperor]], [[Kaika Emperor]] | clan = Ancestor of [[Mononobe no Muraji]] | parents = Father: [[Ōyaguchi no Sukune no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson">"Tenson Honki," in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.</ref>; Mother: Sakadoyura no Hime<ref name="tenson"/> | siblings = [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/>; [[Utsushikome no Mikoto|Utsushikome]]<ref name="tenson"/>; [[Ōmine no Onime no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/> | spouse = Takaya Awara Hime<ref name="tenson"/> | children = [[Ikashikoo no Mikoto]], [[Ikashikome no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/> }}
'''Ōhesoki no Mikoto''' (大綜麻杵命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese aristocrat and the ancestor of the {{ill|Mononobe no Muraji|ja|物部氏}}. He is also referred to as Ōsoki no Mikoto in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and as Ōhesosoki no Mikoto in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}.
== Overview == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Ōhesoki is noted as the father of [[Ikashikome no Mikoto]], the mother of [[Sujin Emperor]]. While his other activities are not extensively recorded in the *Nihon Shoki*, the *Sendai Kuji Hongi*'s "Tenson Honki" section recounts that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen's reign, Ōhesoki was appointed as Ōne, a high-ranking ministerial position, and his brother [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]] became Ōomi (Minister). He later became Ōomi under Emperor Kaika in the 8th year of his reign.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, he is identified as the grandfather of Tatetsukawa no Mikoto, indicating his importance in early genealogy.
Although the early genealogy of the Mononobe clan is somewhat inconsistent across historical sources, there is a general agreement that Ōhesoki was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} and a sibling to [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]]<ref>{{Cite book|author=Hoga Toshio|title=Research on Ancient Clans Vol. 8: The Mononobe Clan|publisher=Aogaki Publishing|year=2016|page=104|chapter=Four: Early Movements of the Mononobe Clan}}</ref>.
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Mononobe no Muraji]] * [[List of figures before the Asuka period]]

He later became Ōomi under Emperor Kaika in the 8th year of his reign.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}, he is identified as the grandfather of Tatetsukawa no Mikoto, indicating his importance in early genealogy.
Although the early genealogy of the Mononobe clan is somewhat inconsistent across historical sources, there is a general agreement that Ōhesoki was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=draft}} and a sibling to [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]]<ref>{{Cite book|author=Hoga Toshio|title=Research on Ancient Clans Vol. 8: The Mononobe Clan|publisher=Aogaki Publishing|year=2016|page=104|chapter=Four: Early Movements of the Mononobe Clan}}</ref>.

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
2 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大綜麻杵命 up to rev 88459822
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大綜麻杵命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[弥生時代]]、[[上古]] | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = 大綜麻杵、大綜杵命、太綜杵命、大閉蘇杵命、大閉蘓杵命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 大禰、[[大臣 (古代日本)|大臣]] | 主君 = [[孝元天皇]]、[[開化天皇]] | 氏族 = [[物部氏|物部連]]等祖 | 父母 = 父：[[大矢口宿禰命]]<ref name="tenson">「天孫本紀」『[[先代旧事本紀]]』。</ref>、母：坂戸由良都姫<ref name="tenson"/> | 兄弟 = [[内色許男命]]<ref name="tenson"/>、[[内色許売命]]<ref name="tenson"/>、[[大峯大尼命]]<ref name="tenson"/> | 妻 = 高屋阿波良姫<ref name="tenson"/> | 子 = [[伊香色雄命]]<ref name="tenson"/>、[[伊香色謎命]]<ref name="tenson"/> | 特記事項 = }} '''大綜麻杵命'''（おおへそきのみこと、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]・'''[[物部氏|物部連]]'''の祖。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』では'''大綜杵命'''、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では'''大閉蘇杵命'''とも表記する。
== 概要 == 『[[日本書紀]]』の[[崇神天皇]]紀に崇神天皇の母・[[伊香色謎命]]の父として[[物部氏]]遠祖'''大綜麻杵'''と見える。『日本書紀』ではそれ以外に目立った活動は伝えられないが、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の「天皇本紀」では、[[孝元天皇]]即位8年春正月に[[物部氏|物部連公]]の祖・[[宇摩志麻治命]]の子孫である大綜杵命を大禰とし、兄弟の[[欝色雄命]]を[[大臣 (古代日本)|大臣]]としたことが記される。また、開化天皇即位8年春正月には大臣となったことが伝えられる。
『[[新撰姓氏録]]』左京神別の大宅首条では建新川命の祖父として登場する。
なお、物部氏初期の系譜は史料によって混乱が見られるが、大綜麻杵命については[[饒速日命]]の5世孫で[[内色許男命]]と同一世代（兄弟）とする系譜にはほとんど異説がない<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「四 崇神前代の物部氏の動向」『古代氏族の研究⑧ 物部氏 剣神奉斎の軍事大族』青垣出版、2016年、104頁。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[物部氏]] * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおへそきのみこと}} [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:物部氏]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大綜麻杵命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|WD=Q55536799}}; {{ill|Ikagashikome|WD=Q10885176}}|father={{ill|Q106106001|WD=Q106106001}}; {{ill|Q108933348|WD=Q108933348}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q106106718]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble family | name = Ōhesoki no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōhesoki, Ōsoki no Mikoto, Taesoki no Mikoto, Ōhesosoki no Mikoto, Ōhesozoki no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_titles = Ōne (大禰), [[Minister (ancient Japan)|Ōomi]] | lord = [[Kōgen Emperor]], [[Kaika Emperor]] | clan = Ancestor of [[Mononobe no Muraji]] | parents = Father: [[Ōyaguchi no Sukune no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson">"Tenson Honki," in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]].</ref>; Mother: Sakadoyura no Hime<ref name="tenson"/> | siblings = [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/>; [[Utsushikome no Mikoto|Utsushikome]]<ref name="tenson"/>; [[Ōmine no Onime no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/> | spouse = Takaya Awara Hime<ref name="tenson"/> | children = [[Ikashikoo no Mikoto]], [[Ikashikome no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/> }}
'''Ōhesoki no Mikoto''' (大綜麻杵命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese aristocrat and the ancestor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]. He is also referred to as Ōsoki no Mikoto in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and as Ōhesosoki no Mikoto in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Overview == In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Ōhesoki is noted as the father of [[Ikashikome no Mikoto]], the mother of [[:en:Sujin Emperor|Sujin Emperor]]. While his other activities are not extensively recorded in the *Nihon Shoki*, the *Sendai Kuji Hongi*'s "Tenson Honki" section recounts that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen's reign, Ōhesoki was appointed as Ōne, a high-Ranking ministerial position, and his brother [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]] became Ōomi (Minister). He later became Ōomi under Emperor Kaika in the 8th year of his reign.
In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], he is identified as the grandfather of Tatetsukawa no Mikoto, indicating his importance in early genealogy.
Although the early genealogy of the Mononobe clan is somewhat inconsistent across historical sources, there is a general agreement that Ōhesoki was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ|1=Nigihayahi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10949054}} and a sibling to [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]]<ref>{{Cite book|author=Hoga Toshio|title=Research on Ancient Clans Vol. 8: The Mononobe Clan|publisher=Aogaki Publishing|year=2016|page=104|chapter=Four: Early Movements of the Mononobe Clan}}</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大綜麻杵命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|WD=Q55536799}}; {{ill|Ikagashikome|WD=Q10885176}}|father={{ill|Q106106001|WD=Q106106001}}; {{ill|Q108933348|WD=Q108933348}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q106106718]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble family | name = Ōhesoki no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōhesoki, Ōsoki no Mikoto, Taesoki no Mikoto, Ōhesosoki no Mikoto, Ōhesozoki no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_titles = Ōne (大禰), [[Minister (ancient Japan)|Ōomi]] | lord = [[Kōgen Emperor]], [[Kaika Emperor]] | clan = Ancestor of [[Mononobe no Muraji]] | parents = Father: [[Ōyaguchi no Sukune no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson">"Tenson Honki," in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]].</ref>; Mother: Sakadoyura no Hime<ref name="tenson"/> | siblings = [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/>; [[Utsushikome no Mikoto|Utsushikome]]<ref name="tenson"/>; [[Ōmine no Onime no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/> | spouse = Takaya Awara Hime<ref name="tenson"/> | children = [[Ikashikoo no Mikoto]], [[Ikashikome no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/> }}
'''Ōhesoki no Mikoto''' (大綜麻杵命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese aristocrat and the ancestor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]. He is also referred to as Ōsoki no Mikoto in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and as Ōhesosoki no Mikoto in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Overview == In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Ōhesoki is noted as the father of [[Ikashikome no Mikoto]], the mother of [[:en:Sujin Emperor|Sujin Emperor]]. While his other activities are not extensively recorded in the *Nihon Shoki*, the *Sendai Kuji Hongi*'s "Tenson Honki" section recounts that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen's reign, Ōhesoki was appointed as Ōne, a high-Ranking ministerial position, and his brother [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]] became Ōomi (Minister). He later became Ōomi under Emperor Kaika in the 8th year of his reign.
In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], he is identified as the grandfather of Tatetsukawa no Mikoto, indicating his importance in early genealogy.
Although the early genealogy of the Mononobe clan is somewhat inconsistent across historical sources, there is a general agreement that Ōhesoki was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ|1=Nigihayahi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10949054}} and a sibling to [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]]<ref>{{Cite book|author=Hoga Toshio|title=Research on Ancient Clans Vol. 8: The Mononobe Clan|publisher=Aogaki Publishing|year=2016|page=104|chapter=Four: Early Movements of the Mononobe Clan}}</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大綜麻杵命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Ikaga Shikoo|WD=Q55536799}}; {{ill|Ikagashikome|WD=Q10885176}}|father={{ill|Q106106001|WD=Q106106001}}; {{ill|Q108933348|WD=Q108933348}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q106106718]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble family | name = Ōhesoki no Mikoto | image = | caption = | period = [[Yayoi period]], [[Ancient Japan]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Ōhesoki, Ōsoki no Mikoto, Taesoki no Mikoto, Ōhesosoki no Mikoto, Ōhesozoki no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_titles = Ōne (大禰), [[Minister (ancient Japan)|Ōomi]] | lord = [[Kōgen Emperor]], [[Kaika Emperor]] | clan = Ancestor of [[Mononobe no Muraji]] | parents = Father: [[Ōyaguchi no Sukune no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson">"Tenson Honki," in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]].</ref>; Mother: Sakadoyura no Hime<ref name="tenson"/> | siblings = [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/>; [[Utsushikome no Mikoto|Utsushikome]]<ref name="tenson"/>; [[Ōmine no Onime no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/> | spouse = Takaya Awara Hime<ref name="tenson"/> | children = [[Ikashikoo no Mikoto]], [[Ikashikome no Mikoto]]<ref name="tenson"/> }}
'''Ōhesoki no Mikoto''' (大綜麻杵命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese aristocrat and the ancestor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]. He is also referred to as Ōsoki no Mikoto in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and as Ōhesosoki no Mikoto in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Overview == In the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Ōhesoki is noted as the father of [[Ikashikome no Mikoto]], the mother of [[:en:Sujin Emperor|Sujin Emperor]]. While his other activities are not extensively recorded in the *Nihon Shoki*, the *Sendai Kuji Hongi*'s "Tenson Honki" section recounts that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen's reign, Ōhesoki was appointed as Ōne, a high-Ranking ministerial position, and his brother [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]] became Ōomi (Minister). He later became Ōomi under Emperor Kaika in the 8th year of his reign.
In the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]], he is identified as the grandfather of Tatetsukawa no Mikoto, indicating his importance in early genealogy.
Although the early genealogy of the Mononobe clan is somewhat inconsistent across historical sources, there is a general agreement that Ōhesoki was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ|1=Nigihayahi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10949054}} and a sibling to [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]]<ref>{{Cite book|author=Hoga Toshio|title=Research on Ancient Clans Vol. 8: The Mononobe Clan|publisher=Aogaki Publishing|year=2016|page=104|chapter=Four: Early Movements of the Mononobe Clan}}</ref>.

== Overview == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Ōhesoki is noted as the father of [[Ikashikome no Mikoto]], the mother of [[:en:Sujin Emperor|Sujin Emperor]]. While his other activities are not extensively recorded in the *Nihon Shoki*, the *Sendai Kuji Hongi*'s "Tenson Honki" section recounts that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen's reign, Ōhesoki was appointed as Ōne, a high-Ranking ministerial position, and his brother [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]] became Ōomi (Minister). He later became Ōomi under Emperor Kaika in the 8th year of his reign.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, he is identified as the grandfather of Tatetsukawa no Mikoto, indicating his importance in early genealogy.
Although the early genealogy of the Mononobe clan is somewhat inconsistent across historical sources, there is a general agreement that Ōhesoki was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ|1=Nigihayahi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10949054}} and a sibling to [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]]<ref>{{Cite book|author=Hoga Toshio|title=Research on Ancient Clans Vol. 8: The Mononobe Clan|publisher=Aogaki Publishing|year=2016|page=104|chapter=Four: Early Movements of the Mononobe Clan}}</ref>.

'''Ōhesoki no Mikoto''' (大綜麻杵命, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese aristocrat and the ancestor of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe no Muraji|1=Mononobe clan}}. He is also referred to as Ōsoki no Mikoto in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} and as Ōhesosoki no Mikoto in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}.
== Overview == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Ōhesoki is noted as the father of [[Ikashikome no Mikoto]], the mother of [[:en:Sujin Emperor|Sujin Emperor]]. While his other activities are not extensively recorded in the *Nihon Shoki*, the *Sendai Kuji Hongi*'s "Tenson Honki" section recounts that in the 8th year of Emperor Kōgen's reign, Ōhesoki was appointed as Ōne, a high-Ranking ministerial position, and his brother [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]] became Ōomi (Minister). He later became Ōomi under Emperor Kaika in the 8th year of his reign.
In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, he is identified as the grandfather of Tatetsukawa no Mikoto, indicating his importance in early genealogy.
Although the early genealogy of the Mononobe clan is somewhat inconsistent across historical sources, there is a general agreement that Ōhesoki was a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ|1=Nigihayahi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10949054}} and a sibling to [[Utsushikome no Mikoto]]<ref>{{Cite book|author=Hoga Toshio|title=Research on Ancient Clans Vol. 8: The Mononobe Clan|publisher=Aogaki Publishing|year=2016|page=104|chapter=Four: Early Movements of the Mononobe Clan}}</ref>.
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Mononobe no Muraji]] * [[List of figures before the Asuka period]]
